title: Never really been in love + Vietnamese-host-family rip-off (Sept 4 2022, Hanoi-era)
html:
TL;DR

  • Sept 4, 2022 note opening with a flat declaration — "I have never really been in love" — and immediately moving to a research question: "I need to find out about my love language and what that even means. What is the study behind it."
  • Bottom of the note, time-stamped 10:20pm in Vietnam: "most likely about to get fucked over by the Vietnamese family that helped me" — Yahir registering in real time that the host family he had been staying with was about to extract from him.
  • Sits at the end of the summer-2022 Vietnam stretch (2022 07 31 vietnam route prep, 2022 09 08 theincung cave) just before he flies back to Corvallis for sophomore year — the trip that book-ends his pre-OSIG identity.
  • The juxtaposition is the note: introspection about the absence of romantic experience and a real-time financial-betrayal countdown stacked into the same six lines.
  • Earliest in-vault appearance of the "love language" investigation — a research-it-don't-feel-it move that recurs in later 2024–2025 notes about Emily and Jo.

Key fragments

"I have never really been in love."

"I need to find out about my love language and what that even means."

"10;20 most likely about to get fucked over by the Vietnamese family that helped me"

Why it matters

This note is two distinct vault threads sharing a page:

  1. Romantic-identity baseline — flat self-report in late 2022 that he hasn't been in love. Useful baseline for the Emily arc (2025), the Jo thread (2023), and any future explore-question about Yahir's love-life chronology.
  2. Vietnam host-family rupture — the only direct evidence in the vault that the trust relationship with the host family broke down. Pair with 2022 07 31 vietnam route prep and 2022 09 08 theincung cave to anchor the Vietnam summer cluster.

Open questions

  • Which Vietnamese host family — was it the one in Ha Giang, Sapa, or Vu Van Ba's orbit? (No name in the note.)
  • What did "fucked over" actually look like — overcharged for stay, asked for a loan, something else? Worth checking adjacent notes.
  • Did Yahir actually research love language frameworks, or did this stay a one-line research question?

Wikilinks
